description:
Using the BFI-25 personality data (BFI25.csv, already open in Jamovi), investigate whether personality traits predict participant age beyond gender.

Reverse-code items E1 and E2 by computing new variables E1r = 7 - E1 and E2r = 7 - E2. Compute two personality scale scores: Extra = MEAN(E1r, E2r, E3, E4, E5) and Neuro = MEAN(N1, N2, N3, N4, N5). Set gender as a nominal variable.

Run a Cronbach's alpha reliability analysis on the Extraversion scale items (E1r, E2r, E3, E4, E5) with both Cronbach's alpha and McDonald's omega enabled.

Run a hierarchical linear regression predicting age. Block 1: gender alone. Block 2: gender + Extra + Neuro. Enable standardized estimates, confidence intervals, collinearity statistics (VIF), and the Durbin-Watson test. Save the residuals from this regression as a new variable, then run descriptive statistics on the residuals with a Shapiro-Wilk normality test and Q-Q plot.

Save the project as Personality_Age.omv in ~/Documents/Jamovi/. Write a report to ~/Documents/Jamovi/age_analysis_report.txt containing: Extraversion Cronbach's alpha, Block 1 R-squared, Block 2 R-squared, R-squared change, the overall model F-statistic and p-value for Block 2, the standardized beta coefficients for Extra and Neuro, VIF values for Extra and Neuro, Durbin-Watson d statistic, and the Shapiro-Wilk W statistic and p-value for the residuals.
